channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: North Korea says it will deploy new artillery guns targeting Seoul
item.description: North Korea says it'll deploy new long-range artillery systems capable of striking the capital region of rival South Korea this year and commission its first naval destroyer in coming weeks.
item.content:encoded:
North Korea says it'll deploy new long-range artillery systems capable of striking the capital region of rival South Korea this year and commission its first naval destroyer in coming weeks.

(Image credit: Korean Central News Agency/Korea News Service)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. military says it intercepted Iranian attacks on 3 Navy ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. military also says that it "targeted Iranian military facilities responsible for attacking U.S. forces." The exchange occurred Thursday as U.S. Navy destroyers transited the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Central Command said in a social media post.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military also says that it "targeted Iranian military facilities responsible for attacking U.S. forces." The exchange occurred Thursday as U.S. Navy destroyers transited the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Central Command said in a social media post.

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat/ISNA)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Former U.S. analyst, once jailed for spying for Israel, to run for Israeli parliament
item.description: Jonathan Pollard, a former U.S. intelligence analyst who served 30 years for spying for Israel, says he will run in the upcoming Israeli elections.
item.content:encoded: Jonathan Pollard, a former U.S. intelligence analyst who served 30 years for spying for Israel, says he will run in the upcoming Israeli elections.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Rubio visits Vatican amid escalating tensions between Trump and Pope Leo
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio traveled to Rome in an effort to preserve a crucial relationship between Washington and the Holy See.
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio traveled to Rome in an effort to preserve a crucial relationship between Washington and the Holy See.

(Image credit: Vatican Media via AP)
item.dc:creator: Claire Giangrave

item.title: China gives suspended death sentences to 2 ex-defense ministers
item.description: Both were the latest to be sentenced in President Xi Jinping's ongoing anti-corruption campaign, which started more than a decade ago.
item.content:encoded: Both were the latest to be sentenced in President Xi Jinping's ongoing anti-corruption campaign, which started more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran reviews U.S. proposal. And, Rubio to meet Pope Leo after Trump's criticism
item.description: Iran is reviewing the Trump administration's latest proposal to end the war. And, Secretary of State Marco Rubio is meeting Pope Leo XIV amid President Trump's harsh criticism of him.
item.content:encoded:
Iran is reviewing the Trump administration's latest proposal to end the war. And, Secretary of State Marco Rubio is meeting Pope Leo XIV amid President Trump's harsh criticism of him.

(Image credit: Andrew Medichini)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Dirty nickel: The cost of mining in Indonesia
item.description: Across six locations in Indonesia, NPR spoke with locals about how nickel mining is changing the land and daily life. It's brought jobs, but also concerns about environmental damage and public health.
item.content:encoded:
Across six locations in Indonesia, NPR spoke with locals about how nickel mining is changing the land and daily life. It's brought jobs, but also concerns about environmental damage and public health.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton

item.title: Health officials track dozens who left hantavirus-stricken ship after 1st fatality
item.description: Oceanwide Expeditions has revealed that 30 passengers disembarked from its cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak on April 24, on the remote island of St. Helena, nearly two weeks after the first passenger died.
item.content:encoded:
Oceanwide Expeditions has revealed that 30 passengers disembarked from its cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak on April 24, on the remote island of St. Helena, nearly two weeks after the first passenger died.

(Image credit: Misper Apawu)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Affected by the Iran war, residents in Southeast Asian countries voice their concerns
item.description: Southeast Asian countries are voicing concern about President Trump and the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: Southeast Asian countries are voicing concern about President Trump and the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Michael Sullivan

item.title: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with Pope Leo after Trump's criticism
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with Pope Leo after critical comments by President Trump.
item.content:encoded: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with Pope Leo after critical comments by President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams
